channel.title: Comments for page "International Branches Hub"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "International Branches Hub"

item.title: Mod Post
item.content:encoded: Noting that due to a request by the original author on its home branch, SCP-CN-994 has been deleted.

item.title: Season 1 finished
item.content:encoded:
Season 1 of the INT-Hub is now complete. The branches' staff now reevaluates this season and starts to plan Season 2.

Feedback of any sort is welcome.

To clarify: Everybody is invited to correct errors in translations on the INT wiki (what you see here is a mirrored version of the article on the INT wiki in almost all cases). There is no necessity to ask for permission. If you don't have time, pointing out specific errors is very welcome.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It must be the same people who "criticize" lolfoundation, while they enjoy SCP-3213.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't see big changes in the EN-Canon, besides of the branch quirks, like "O4-Concel" form the German and "S5-Concel" form the Italian Branch. ^^

And naturally the personal.

item.title: Re: Thanks, I hate it
item.content:encoded:
And we translate them, so everyone can read them.
If someone dislikes "parallel SCP-Foundation Canons" in form of SCP-[Language Code], you can deny there existences in your head canon and read only "SCP-EN".
This is an offering from INT, to have new reading material.

item.title: Re: SCP-INT?
item.content:encoded: You will see soon :p

item.title: Re: Thanks, I hate it
item.content:encoded:
I disagree with this for a number of reasons:

- having a separate section for a set of articles doesn't link them with being second class - if anything, I see a stronger argument for a separate section making articles seem more prestigious than they should be (see the infamous Heritage Collection)
- they still have to survive the normal deletion process, and the section they're posted in doesn't make a difference (appearing on the mainlist doesn't save you from downvotes)
- making posting to the -EN mainlist the 'final' goal for international articles could be interpreted as reinforcing the superiority of the -EN branch, making all the other -INT branches second class instead of just a few articles. You could argue this is reasonable enough with the -EN branch being the first, largest and most developed, but I imagine the goal of the -INT branches is to reduce that, not reinforce it, and -EN should try to discourage it as far as possible (without lowering its own quality standards - in which case I think this parallel posting system is a good compromise)

item.title: Re: Thanks, I hate it
item.content:encoded: We can't translate them so fast and some can't even english.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
077-DE is not so hight too…
I think EN user don't like the style.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
See them as "Serie 1" SCiPs ^^
Or maybe you don't like the style. They come from very different countries, the people(s) like different stuff than in America and England.

item.title: SCP-INT?
item.content:encoded: What is with SCP-INTs?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What was the problem with SCP-ES-061? I recently read it and I didn't see anything wrong with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As SCP-ES-061 has received massive negative votes and responses we have removed it from this Wiki. It will be replaced as soon as a replacement is found.

item.title: Re: Thanks, I hate it
item.content:encoded: i've read plenty of es stuff and i thought it was pretty good

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's partially due to the time when they were written. A lot of the articles in INT were made before 2015, and the long stories are also hard to translate due to the time it takes to finish them.

item.title: Thanks, I hate it
item.content:encoded:
While I get this is just a compliation from another project, I still really, really don't like having these here on the main site (like this). It was better when articles were directly translated to the mainlist and made to ward off for themselves. Yeah, some of those articles wouldn't survive on their own merits, but that's just because sometimes standards still haven't developed as much in some of the communities (I know for a fact that's the case with ES branch).

Keeping them separate is just, in my humble opinion, a not so subtle "othering". They're effectively made "second class" articles.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know, I've been kind of disappointed by the overall quality of the posted works. I don't know if its the translations or the articles themselves, but a good chunk of them just feel lacking.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: thirding! It’s really popular and well known but has never really gotten a full official translation which is a shame.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP-040-JP is my alltime favorite from that branch. Seconding this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't wait for SCP-040-JP to be translated. That's a big meme around the Japanese community.

item.title: A dumb question...
item.content:encoded: but how to post an article in the related brunch list?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The first round has finished, we continue with the second round on Sunday 14.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good stuff.

Incidentally, until now, I thought all the branches just used a straight national flag logo background (not that there's anything wrong with that) but JP/PL/CN have some really nice design going on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Out of curiosity, wouldn't it be easier to tag all the incoming -INT articles with an int/international tag as opposed to their respective branch's nationality? That way there's an easier way to search for them via tag as opposed to only having one or two articles with an RU or TH tag.
Take what I say with a grain of salt, I am not a tagging expert. I just had a thought and wanted to share it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I excitedly await the release of these articles.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Always love to see the difference between works by people from different cultural backgrounds

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is really cool to see. I'm eagerly awaiting the rest of the translations to come.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is literally the neatest thing I've heard of on here in years. Yes, please do this, I am completely excited for this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
/raises a glass

Hurrah for the International Branches!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: international stuff is so cool! I’m the kind of person that considers all of the international stuff canon because it introduces more stuff, and I love incredibly huge expansive universe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hype so strong it transcends language barriers

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For years the branches of the Foundation not only have translated content from English to other languages but also created original articles; some have just a few articles, some have over 1000. So 1¾ year ago, the branches founded the International Translation Archive, as a platform to upload translations of their work in English, to reach a wider audience and to make translations to other branches easier. Now it's time to share our translations with you.

In the next weeks, we will upload 2 articles a day to this hub until all locked places are filled. After that, we will continue to upload content, at a pace that depends on our experiences during the initial phase.
For those who like to time uploading of their articles well: We most commonly will post in the CET-morning, so at night in America.

As the articles we show here have been translated by non-native English speakers, they probably still contain spelling, grammar or expression errors. Feel free to point those out in the comments, as well as to give general feedback.

Credits: SCP-branch Icons and lock-badges based on the original SCP Logo by far2, the logo designs have been made by several people. Vector graphics of all images can be requested from Dr_Grom

Branch personnel, please be aware that INT staff selects the choice of articles that goes to this hub. Please do not add anything by yourself. If you want an article shown here please talk to INT staff or your branches Representatives.
